thesis: AI infrastructure company building GPU-powered data centers. Up to $46B in contracted revenue from Meta ($27B) and Microsoft ($19.4B) over 5 years. FY2024 revenue $117.5M (+462% YoY); Q4 2025 revenue $228M (+547% YoY). ARR reached $1.2B at end 2025. NVIDIA invested $2B strategically. Extreme overbought conditions with RSI at 84.86 after +616% annual run. Customer concentration (Meta+MSFT = ~100% of contracted revenue) and execution risk are primary concerns.

drivers: Meta and Microsoft contracts ramp to $5B+ annual revenue by 2027
Becomes top-3 AI infrastructure provider globally

risks: GPU deployment delays push revenue recognition back
Competition from CoreWeave or hyperscalers intensifies

base_case.assumptions: Revenue reaches $3.0–3.5B by 2026. No major new contracts beyond Meta/MSFT. Margins improve but stay below hyperscaler levels. Multiple settles at ~15x sales.

key_risks: Customer concentration — Meta + Microsoft = ~100% of contracted revenue
Execution risk — building 22,000 GPU data centers is enormously complex
Valuation risk — trading at ~76x TTM sales with $40B+ market cap
GPU supply risk — dependent on NVIDIA for latest chips
Geopolitical risk — Russian origins create sanctions/headline risk
RSI at 84.86 — extremely overbought; pullback likely
